content:
📜 2. Faction-Wide Consequences for Failed Secret Ballot Rituals

📜 If a faction proposes a ritual that the class votes against, the entire faction suffers a penalty.

✔ How Failed Secret Ballots Work:
• If a ritual proposal is denied by secret ballot, the initiator’s entire faction takes a debuff.
• If multiple factions support the failed ritual, all involved factions suffer.
• If a faction leader proposed the ritual, the penalty is even worse.

✔ Example Faction-Wide Penalties:
• The Mark of Rejection → The faction cannot propose another ritual for a set period.
• The Scar of Defiance → The faction’s members gain an individual debuff (ex: difficulty with persuasion, weakened skill checks).
• The Wages of Hubris → The faction leader is temporarily stripped of their leadership status and must re-earn it.

📜 Why This Works:
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 548 (chunk 548/555)
